summary: Full backend + frontend testing of PureNorth Städ. 14 backend pytest tests written (13/14 pass after fix; 1 was test-logic issue, fixed). Frontend home page + booking form work correctly (all validation paths, success state, Swedish content). CRITICAL bug found in Admin dashboard: API returns booking ID as '_id' (Pydantic alias) but Admin.jsx and all admin endpoints in the UI expect 'id'. As a result, every booking row's data-testid renders as 'booking-row-undefined', status select is 'booking-status-undefined', and PATCH/DELETE calls go to '/api/bookings/undefined/status' returning 500 — status update and delete are completely broken in the UI. React also logs 'unique key' warnings because b.id is undefined.

critical.issue: Response serializes id field as '_id' (Pydantic alias) instead of 'id'. Frontend uses b.id everywhere -> all booking row keys/testids/status/delete actions are 'undefined'. Fix: set response_model_by_alias=False on endpoints OR use Booking.model_dump(by_alias=False) OR remove alias on the id field. Verified via curl returns key '_id'.

ui_bugs.issue: data-testid renders as 'booking-row-undefined', 'booking-status-undefined', 'booking-delete-undefined' because backend returns '_id' not 'id'. Tests targeting booking-status-{id}/booking-delete-{id} will fail. Also React console warning: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' in Dashboard.
PATCH /api/bookings/undefined/status returns 500, toast 'Kunde inte uppdatera status' appears. Same will apply to delete. Root cause = same as above (_id vs id).

integration_issues.issue: Broken end-to-end because booking ID is missing in the frontend model field b.id. Will be fixed when backend returns 'id' instead of '_id'.

issues: Browser tab title is generic 'Emergent | Fullstack App' — should be branded (e.g., 'PureNorth Städ — Städfirma i Umeå') in /app/frontend/public/index.html

action_items: CRITICAL: Make backend booking responses use 'id' instead of '_id'. Recommended fix in /app/backend/server.py: on POST/GET /api/bookings endpoints, return doc with key 'id' explicitly (e.g., add response_model_by_alias=False to @api_router.post/get decorators, or rebuild the dict without alias). After fix, Admin Dashboard status update + delete will start working and the React key warning will disappear.
Update <title> in /app/frontend/public/index.html to PureNorth Städ branding (Swedish).
critical_code_review_comments: Booking Pydantic model uses alias='_id' on the id field but endpoints return raw model_dump (default by_alias=True) — that's the root cause of the admin breakage. Either drop the alias or set response_model_by_alias=False.
Admin.jsx Dashboard imports useNavigate but never uses it (unused import).
server.py uses deprecated FastAPI on_event hooks — consider lifespan, but not blocking.
Frontend stores JWT in localStorage (XSS risk). Acceptable for MVP; backend also supports cookie auth (defense in depth).
Update status endpoint accepts any string for status — no validation that it is one of {'new','contacted','done'}.

success_rate.frontend: 70% — public site + booking form + admin login OK; admin status update + delete BROKEN due to _id/id mismatch

seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_ prefixed bookings during testing; cleanup test removes them.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Once main agent fixes the _id->id serialization in /app/backend/server.py (POST/GET /api/bookings), re-run /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and re-run the admin dashboard Playwright flow that selects booking-status-{id} and booking-delete-{id} to confirm status update and delete work end-to-end. Login/booking-create/validation flows are already verified.
rca_of_the_issue: Pydantic Booking model: id: Optional[PyObjectId] = Field(default=None, alias='_id'). FastAPI default response_model_by_alias=True ⇒ JSON key is '_id'. Frontend Admin.jsx reads b.id (undefined) ⇒ b.id is interpolated into data-testid and url paths as 'undefined' ⇒ PATCH/DELETE return 500 'Object id ... is not a valid ObjectId' and React emits missing-key warning. Mitigation: add response_model_by_alias=False on the affected endpoints, or change Field alias to populate-only, or return Booking.model_dump(by_alias=False).
